title: apargia hispida subsp. hastilis
intro: Apargia hispida subsp. hastilis, also known as a sub-species of Argentinean thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the genus Apargia and is native to the southern regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular choice for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Apargia hispida subsp. hastilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in a topical cream or ointment to reap its medicinal benefits.
